title: Centralize secrets in a vault or secret manager
description: Store secrets in a dedicated manager with access control and audit, and have services fetch them at runtime rather than baking them in.
body: Use when an application needs credentials across environments. Do not scatter secrets across config files, CI variables, and images. Actions: store each secret in the vault with least-privilege access policies; have services authenticate and fetch at startup or on demand; enable audit logging of access. Validation signals include code containing no secret values and the vault's audit log showing scoped access. Risk boundary: broad vault access defeats the purpose, so scope policies tightly, never log fetched values, and escalate any request to widen access for review.
